essence: Inspects, tests, and maintains fire hydrants to ensure reliable water supply for firefighting operations.

identity.title: Fire Hydrant Technician
identity.summary: Checks, repairs, and certifies fire hydrants, ensuring they provide adequate pressure and flow for emergency firefighting.

salary.notes: Technicians covering large municipal networks or specialist industrial hydrant systems can exceed £40k-£45k+.

qualifications: Training in hydrant inspection and maintenance
Knowledge of water‑supply systems and fire‑service requirements
Ability to test flow, pressure, and valve integrity
Understanding of safety protocols and compliance standards

lifestyle.description: Fire hydrant technicians travel across neighbourhoods, industrial sites, and public areas inspecting and maintaining hydrants. The role involves testing water flow, checking valves, repairing faults, and ensuring hydrants are accessible and compliant. It suits individuals who enjoy practical outdoor work, independence, and supporting firefighting readiness.

habits: Tests hydrants for flow and pressure
Keeps accurate inspection and maintenance records
Ensures hydrants remain accessible and unobstructed
Follows strict safety and compliance procedures
Communicates clearly with fire services and water authorities
